This family is a part of the larger order Asparagales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes agaves, asparagus, and orchids. Amaryllidaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Amaryllis lineata belonging to the subfamily Amaryllidoideae. The subfamily Amaryllidoideae is characterized by its large, showy flowers and includes many popular ornamental plants such as the Christmas lily and the Easter lily. Amaryllis lineata is a relatively rare species within this family, known for its unique characteristics and distribution.
